title: Econometrics
text:
Econometrics
Time Series Analysis

Welcome!

This website is built around the slides from my lectures, with the full course narrative woven around them so that the site also works as a self-contained reference for the module.
Throughout, you’ll find animations, live R graphs, audio snippets, signposts to textbooks and other resources where relevant.
If there’s any concept you’d like more support with, let me know. The syllabus is fixed, but I’m happy to add supplementary materials like an audio snippet or a screen recording where helpful.

Ragvir




Topic 01
Introducing Time Series Analysis
Core statistical foundations, what time series analysis is, and why it needs special techniques.

Read notes → PDF ↓



Topic 02
Introducing ARMA(p, q) models
White noise, MA and AR building blocks, the lag operator, and the full ARMA(p, q) family.

Read notes → PDF ↓



Topic 03
Measuring dependence
Autocovariance and autocorrelation functions — how we quantify dependence across time.

Read notes → PDF ↓



Topic 04
Defining stationarity
Strong and weak stationarity, and the autocovariance structure of stationary series.

Read notes → PDF ↓



Coming soon
More topics
Further topics will be added here as the module progresses.
